instruction: Classify the meter/grid event description into an event type.
baseline_prompt: You are a smart-grid operations assistant. Classify the meter/grid event description into an event type. Classify into one of these categories: outage, overconsumption, tamper, voltage_anomaly, billing_dispute, maintenance.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Emergency call logged: entire apartment complex on Riverside Drive experiencing total blackout. Zero consumption recorded from all 156 connected meters since 6:42 PM.
Commercial account #CC-9012 registered 340 kWh consumption between 11 PM and 6 AM last night, representing a 450% increase compared to their average overnight usage of 62 kWh for the same period.

Security alert: Meter housing at 156 Oak Street shows evidence of unauthorized access. Internal wiring has been bypassed around the current sensor, and usage data exhibits unexplained gaps during peak hours.

Automated monitoring detected voltage fluctuations between 185V and 245V over a 15-minute window at feeder F-12. These rapid swings outside the acceptable band could damage customer equipment and need urgent attention.
Tenant at unit 4B challenges the surcharge of $95 added to their account, argues they never authorized any service upgrades and demands explanation for the unexpected fees on this cycle's statement.

Annual calibration required for all revenue meters in district 7. Field technicians to verify accuracy against certified standards and update firmware to latest version. Planning window: next 30 days.
Substation breaker CB-12 tripped offline at 11:23, resulting in complete service interruption to industrial park zone. All metering devices unresponsive.

Residential smart meter #SMR-44291 reporting sustained draw of 18.5 kW since yesterday evening, which is inconsistent with a single-family home's expected load profile and 6x higher than neighbor average.

Detected irregular magnetic field interference near the measurement components of unit B-2341. Current readings are showing approximately 40% of expected values based on historical load profile for this commercial facility.

Multiple meters in the downtown grid sector are reporting simultaneous voltage depression events, with readings showing drops to 195-205V range when normal delivery should be 220-240V. Load balancing or supply issues suspected.
Account holder reports their smart meter was replaced mid-cycle on the 18th, but the final invoice appears to double-count usage for a week-long overlap period resulting in an inflated total.
Smart meter communication gateway experiencing intermittent connectivity. Network diagnostics indicate antenna replacement recommended. Scheduling field visit to replace hardware component.
Distribution line fault detected in grid segment G4-NW. Complete cessation of power delivery to 203 endpoints. Automatic meter readings unavailable due to loss of supply.

Apartment building transformer showing unusual strain with aggregate demand reaching 67% above forecasted levels for this time of day. Individual meter analysis reveals unit 12C pulling 22 kW continuously for past 3 hours versus typical 3 kW average.

Physical inspection reveals the seal on meter #4782 has been broken and the cover shows signs of forced entry. The customer's consumption dropped by 87% over the past two billing cycles despite similar occupancy patterns.

test.query: Phase A voltage at the industrial park substation has been running at 252V consistently since 6 AM, which is above our 240V maximum specification. This could stress insulation and reduce equipment lifespan for connected facilities.

Customer called regarding their latest statement showing $487 for the month of March, claiming they were only home for 10 days during that period and the amount seems excessive compared to their typical $180 monthly charges.

Transformer inspection scheduled for substation 4B on Thursday at 0600 hours. Crew will perform oil sampling, thermal imaging, and bushing checks. Estimated duration 4 hours with partial load transfer to adjacent circuits.
Residential customer indicates all lights and appliances went dark approximately 45 minutes ago. Remote diagnostics show meter has lost grid connection entirely.

Small business account flagged for review: energy consumption yesterday was 1,247 kWh compared to their 30-day rolling average of 198 kWh per day, with no equipment upgrade notifications or facility expansion on record.

Technician report indicates potential shunt device installed across terminals in meter box at 2847 Maple Drive. Recorded energy usage is inconsistent with connected load and time-of-use patterns observed from neighboring comparable units.

Smart meter SM-7742 recorded voltage spikes reaching 268V during the thunderstorm event last night. This exceeds our upper operating limit of 250V and may have affected sensitive electronics in the commercial district.
Consumer disagrees with estimated reading used for February billing after stating they provided actual meter photos on the 28th showing significantly lower consumption than what was invoiced.
Routine breaker testing at primary substation shows contact wear on CB-301. Replacement parts ordered and installation planned for Sunday overnight when demand is lowest.
Transformer station T-447 shows no load activity for the past 2 hours. Downstream meters in coverage area reporting null values and communication failure.

Customer at residential unit 4782 showing instantaneous demand of 47 kW at 2:15 PM, which is approximately 8 times their typical weekday afternoon baseline of 5.8 kW. Historical patterns show no similar peaks in the past 90 days.

Customer premise inspection found evidence that the meter has been inverted and reconnected improperly. Historical data shows negative energy flow indicators and consumption readings that are physically impossible given the service capacity.

The distribution transformer at Oak Street reported sustained low voltage conditions at 201V throughout the afternoon. Normal operating range is 230V ±5%, so this represents a significant deviation requiring immediate investigation.

Business owner from Maple Street location contests the $1,240 assessed for last quarter, insists their solar panel contribution should have offset at least 60% of grid consumption but sees no credit applied.
Distribution pole #8847 identified during patrol with woodpecker damage and early signs of decay at base. Engineering recommends pole replacement before winter storm season begins.
All readings from sector 7B meters have flatlined at zero since 03:15 AM. No power draw detected across 47 residential units in the Oak Street corridor.
Zone 7 substation experiencing localized stress due to one customer premise consuming 540 kWh in a 4-hour period this afternoon. Their monthly average daily consumption is only 45 kWh total.

Anomalous zero-consumption readings during hours when smart home system confirms multiple high-power appliances were actively drawing electricity. Physical inspection scheduled due to suspected mechanical interference with the measurement mechanism.
